'''冠稲荷神社'''（かんむりいなりじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]細谷町にある[[神社]]である。
{{和暦|1125}}に[[源義国]]によって創建され、{{和暦|1333}}に[[新田義貞]]が挙兵の際、社の前で兜（冠）の中に神霊を呼び必勝祈願したことから「冠稲荷」と呼ばれるようになった。
境内に咲く[[木瓜]]の花は有名。
==文化財== *[[算額]] 文化11年（1814年）大川栄信門人大川直信ほか3名奉納 群馬県指定重要文化財
==アクセス== ===自動車=== *[[東北自動車道]][[館林インターチェンジ|館林IC]]より約40分 *[[関越自動車道]][[花園インターチェンジ|花園IC]]より約30分 *[[北関東自動車道]][[伊勢崎インターチェンジ|伊勢崎IC]]より約20分 ===電車=== *[[東武伊勢崎線]] **[[太田駅 (群馬県)|太田駅]]よりタクシーで15分 **[[細谷駅 (群馬県)|細谷駅]]より徒歩20分

'''冠稲荷神社'''（かんむりいなりじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]細谷町にある[[神社]]である。
== 概要 == [[1125年]]（天治2年）に[[源義国]]によって創建され、[[1333年]]（元弘3年、正慶2年）に[[新田義貞]]が挙兵の際、社の前で兜（冠）の中に神霊を呼び必勝祈願したことから「冠稲荷」と呼ばれるようになった。
境内に咲く[[ボケ (植物)|木瓜]]の花は有名。
==文化財== === 群馬県指定文化財 === *[[算額]]：[[文化 (元号)|文化]]11年（[[1814年]]）大川栄信門人大川直信ほか3名奉納
=== 群馬県指定天然記念物 === * 冠稲荷のボケ1株 - 樹高は約3.5ｍで、約500本の樹が分岐して半円形状に叢生し株を形成している。3月中旬から4月上旬にかけて開花し秋に結実する。樹齢は300～400年と推定されている。<ref>昭和30年1月14日指定。群馬県文化財保護条例第8条の規定による。指定等の基準第6の3の（2）のアによる。園芸種の緋ボケでは県内でも古く株も大きい。</ref>
==アクセス== ===自動車=== *[[国道354号]][[東毛広域幹線道路|太田バイパス]]沿い *[[東北自動車道]][[館林インターチェンジ|館林IC]]より約40分 *[[関越自動車道]][[花園インターチェンジ|花園IC]]より約30分 *[[北関東自動車道]][[伊勢崎インターチェンジ|伊勢崎IC]]より約20分
===電車=== *[[東武伊勢崎線]] **[[太田駅 (群馬県)|太田駅]]よりタクシーで15分 **[[細谷駅 (群馬県)|細谷駅]]より徒歩20分

{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんむりいなりしんしや}} [[category:群馬県の神社]] [[category:算額]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]]

== 概要 == [[1125年]]（天治2年）に[[源義国]]によって創建され、[[1333年]]（元弘3年、正慶2年）に[[新田義貞]]が挙兵の際、社の前で兜（冠）の中に神霊を呼び必勝祈願したことから「冠稲荷」と呼ばれるようになった。

=== 群馬県指定天然記念物 === * 冠稲荷のボケ1株 - 樹高は約3.5ｍで、約500本の樹が分岐して半円形状に叢生し株を形成している。3月中旬から4月上旬にかけて開花し秋に結実する。樹齢は300～400年と推定されている。<ref>昭和30年1月14日指定。群馬県文化財保護条例第8条の規定による。指定等の基準第6の3の（2）のアによる。園芸種の緋ボケでは県内でも古く株も大きい。</ref>

'''冠稲荷神社'''（かんむりいなりじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]細谷町にある[[神社]]である。 == 概要 == [[1125年]]（[[天治]]2年）、[[源義国]]による創建と伝わる。
[[1174年]]（[[承安]]4年）、[[源義経]]が奥州に下向する際、源氏ゆかりの社であることを知り、冠の中に勧請してきた[[伏見稲荷大社]]の分霊を鎮祭したとされる。
[[1333年]]（[[元弘]]3年、[[正慶]]2年）、[[新田義貞]]が挙兵の際、社の前で兜（冠）の中に神霊を呼び、必勝祈願した。
これらの故事にちなんで「冠稲荷」、「冠稲荷大明神」と呼ばれるようになった。 境内に咲く県指定天然記念物の[[ボケ (植物)|木瓜]]の花は有名。 ==文化財== === 群馬県指定文化財 === *最上流[[算額]]：[[文化 (元号)|文化]]11年（[[1814年]]）大川栄信門人大川直信ほか3名奉納 === 群馬県指定天然記念物 === * 冠稲荷のボケ1株 - 樹高は約3.5ｍで、約500本の樹が分岐して半円形状に叢生し株を形成している。3月中旬から4月上旬にかけて開花し秋に結実する。樹齢は300～400年と推定されている。昭和30年1月14日指定。群馬県文化財保護条例第8条の規定による。指定等の基準第6の3の（2）のアによる。園芸種の緋ボケでは県内でも古く株も大きい。 ==アクセス== ===自動車=== *[[国道354号]][[東毛広域幹線道路|太田バイパス]]沿い *[[東北自動車道]][[館林インターチェンジ|館林IC]]より約40分 *[[関越自動車道]][[花園インターチェンジ|花園IC]]より約30分 *[[北関東自動車道]][[伊勢崎インターチェンジ|伊勢崎IC]]より約20分 ===電車=== *[[東武伊勢崎線]] **[[太田駅 (群馬県)|太田駅]]よりタクシーで15分 **[[細谷駅 (群馬県)|細谷駅]]より徒歩20分 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kanmuri.com/ 冠稲荷神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんむりいなりしんしや}} [[category:群馬県の神社]] [[category:算額]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 冠稲荷神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[太田市]]細谷町1 |ISO = JP-13 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 16|緯度秒 = 7.69 |経度度 =139|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 5.49 |祭神 = 稲荷大神（[[宇迦之御魂大神]]ほか4柱の総称）など |創建 = [[1125年]]（[[天治]]2年） |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]正面千鳥破風向拝唐破風付 |例祭 = [[初午]]大祭（[[3月]]下旬の日曜日） |神事 = 初午大祭三大神事（稲荷神餅祭、義経公・義貞公厄除稚児行列、細谷冠稲荷獅子舞）、 [[新嘗祭]]（11月23日） |地図 = Japan Gunma }}
'''冠稲荷神社'''（かんむりいなりじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]細谷町にある[[神社]]である。
== 概要 == [[1125年]]（[[天治]]2年）、[[源義国]]による創建と伝わる。
[[1174年]]（[[承安 (日本)|承安]]4年）、[[源義経]]が奥州に下向する際、源氏ゆかりの社であることを知り、冠の中に勧請してきた[[伏見稲荷大社]]の分霊を鎮祭したとされる。
[[1333年]]（[[元弘]]3年、[[正慶]]2年）、[[新田義貞]]が挙兵の際、社の前で兜（冠）の中に神霊を呼び、必勝祈願した。これらの故事にちなんで「冠稲荷」、「冠稲荷大明神」と呼ばれるようになった。
境内に咲く県指定天然記念物の[[ボケ (植物)|木瓜]]の花は有名。
==文化財== === 群馬県指定文化財 === *最上流[[算額]]：[[文化 (元号)|文化]]11年（[[1814年]]）大川栄信門人大川直信ほか3名奉納
=== 群馬県指定天然記念物 === * 冠稲荷のボケ1株 - 樹高約3.5ｍ、根元回り約3mの緋ボケで、約500本の樹が分岐して半円形状に叢生し株を形成している。3月中旬から4月上旬にかけて開花し秋に結実する。樹齢は300～400年と推定されている。<ref>昭和30年1月14日指定。群馬県文化財保護条例第8条の規定による。指定等の基準第6の3の（2）のアによる。園芸種の緋ボケでは県内でも古く株も大きい。</ref>
==アクセス== ===自動車=== *[[国道354号]][[東毛広域幹線道路|太田バイパス]]沿い *[[東北自動車道]][[館林インターチェンジ|館林IC]]より約40分 *[[関越自動車道]][[花園インターチェンジ|花園IC]]より約30分 *[[北関東自動車道]][[伊勢崎インターチェンジ|伊勢崎IC]]より約20分
===電車=== *[[東武伊勢崎線]] **[[太田駅 (群馬県)|太田駅]]よりタクシーで15分 **[[細谷駅 (群馬県)|細谷駅]]より徒歩20分

{{神社 |名称 = 冠稲荷神社 |画像 = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|300px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[太田市]]細谷町1 |ISO = JP-13 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 16|緯度秒 = 7.69 |経度度 =139|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 5.49 |祭神 = 稲荷大神（[[宇迦之御魂大神]]ほか4柱の総称）など |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = [[1125年]]（[[天治]]2年） |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]正面千鳥破風向拝唐破風付 |例祭 = [[初午]]大祭（[[3月]]下旬の日曜日） |神事 = 初午大祭三大神事（稲荷神餅祭、義経公・義貞公厄除稚児行列、細谷冠稲荷獅子舞）、 [[新嘗祭]]（11月23日） |地図 = Japan Gunma |ラベル位置 = left }} '''冠稲荷神社'''（かんむりいなりじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]細谷町にある[[神社]]である。[[稲荷神#日本七社|日本七社]]（日本七稲荷）の一つを称する{{Cite web |url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/ |title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社 |publisher='''冠稲荷神社''' |accessdate=2018-11-26 }}。 == 祭神 == === === *[[宇迦御魂神]] *[[大穴牟遅神]] *[[太田神]] *[[大宮能売神]] *[[保食命]] *[[少彦名神]] *[[応神天皇|品陀和気神]] *[[菊理姫|菊理日売神]] *[[市杵島姫|市杵島毘売神]] *[[大物主神]] *[[菅原道真|菅原道眞公]] *[[建御名方神|健御名方神]] *[[スサノオ|素盞鳴神]] *[[奇稲田姫|奇稲田比賣神]] *[[天照大御神]] *[[祓戸大神|祓戸四神]] *[[薬師如来|薬師菩薩明神]] == 概要 == [[1125年]]（[[天治]]2年）、[[源義国]]による創建と伝わる。 [[1174年]]（[[承安 (日本)|承安]]4年）、[[源義経]]が奥州に下向する際、源氏ゆかりの社であることを知り、冠の中に勧請してきた[[伏見稲荷大社]]の分霊を鎮祭したとされる。 [[1333年]]（[[元弘]]3年、[[正慶]]2年）、[[新田義貞]]が挙兵の際、社の前で兜（冠）の中に神霊を呼び、必勝祈願した。これらの故事にちなんで「冠稲荷」、「冠稲荷大明神」と呼ばれるようになった。 境内に咲く県指定天然記念物の[[ボケ (植物)|木瓜]]の花は有名。 == 境内 == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|甲（きのえ）大鳥居 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|本殿 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|いなり白狐社 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|社務所 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|宝物殿 ==文化財== === 群馬県指定文化財 === *最上流[[算額]]：[[文化 (元号)|文化]]11年（[[1814年]]）大川栄信門人大川直信ほか3名奉納 === 群馬県指定天然記念物 === * 冠稲荷のボケ1株 - 樹高約3.5ｍ、根元回り約3mの緋ボケで、約500本の樹が分岐して半円形状に叢生し株を形成している。3月中旬から4月上旬にかけて開花し秋に結実する。樹齢は300～400年と推定されている。昭和30年1月14日指定。群馬県文化財保護条例第8条の規定による。指定等の基準第6の3の（2）のアによる。園芸種の緋ボケでは県内でも古く株も大きい。 ==アクセス== ===自動車=== *[[国道354号]][[東毛広域幹線道路|太田バイパス]]沿い *[[東北自動車道]][[館林インターチェンジ|館林IC]]より約40分 *[[関越自動車道]][[花園インターチェンジ|花園IC]]より約30分 *[[北関東自動車道]][[伊勢崎インターチェンジ|伊勢崎IC]]より約20分 ===電車=== *[[東武伊勢崎線]] **[[太田駅 (群馬県)|太田駅]]よりタクシーで15分 **[[細谷駅 (群馬県)|細谷駅]]より徒歩20分 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat}} * {{Official website}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんむりいなりしんしや}} [[category:群馬県の神社]] [[category:算額]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]] [[Category:太田市の建築物]] [[Category:群馬県指定天然記念物]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 冠稲荷神社 |画像 = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[太田市]]細谷町1 |ISO = JP-13 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 16|緯度秒 = 7.69 |経度度 =139|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 5.49 |祭神 = {{要出典範囲|稲荷大神（[[宇迦之御魂大神]]ほか4柱の総称）|date=2024年8月}}など |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = [[1125年]]（[[天治]]2年） |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]正面千鳥破風向拝唐破風付 |例祭 = [[初午]]大祭（[[3月]]下旬の日曜日） |神事 = 初午大祭三大神事（稲荷神餅祭、義経公・義貞公厄除稚児行列、細谷冠稲荷獅子舞）、 [[新嘗祭]]（11月23日） |地図 = Japan Gunma |ラベル位置 = left }} '''冠稲荷神社'''（かんむりいなりじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]][[細谷町 (太田市)|細谷町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[稲荷神#日本七社|日本七社]]（日本七稲荷）の一つを称する{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/ |title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社 |publisher='''冠稲荷神社''' |accessdate=2018-11-26 }}。かつては[[養蚕業|養蚕]]の神として崇められ、旧[[社格]]は村社{{Sfn|太田市|1985|pp=42,57-58}}。 == 祭神 == *[[宇迦御魂神]]{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}} *[[大穴牟遅神]] *[[太田神]] *[[大宮能売神]] *[[保食命]] *[[少彦名神]] *[[応神天皇|品陀和気神]] *[[菊理姫|菊理日売神]] *[[市杵島姫|市杵島毘売神]] *[[大物主神]] *[[菅原道真|菅原道眞公]] *[[建御名方神|健御名方神]] *[[スサノオ|素盞鳴神]] *[[奇稲田姫|奇稲田比賣神]] *[[天照大御神]] *[[祓戸大神|祓戸四神]] *[[薬師如来|薬師菩薩明神]] == 概要 == [[1125年]]（[[天治]]2年）、[[源義国]]による創建と伝わる{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。 [[1174年]]（[[承安 (日本)|承安]]4年）、[[源義経]]が奥州に下向する際、源氏ゆかりの社であることを知り、冠の中に勧請してきた[[伏見稲荷大社]]の分霊を鎮祭したことにより冠稲荷と呼ばれるとされる{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|太田市|1985|pp=681-682}}。 [[1333年]]（[[元弘]]3年）、[[新田義貞]]が挙兵の際戦勝祈願を行い、勝利を収めたため重箱獅子を奉納、[[キンモクセイ]]を植え、[[神領]]を寄進したという{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。 境内に咲く県指定天然記念物の[[ボケ (植物)|木瓜]]の花は有名。 == 境内 == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|甲（きのえ）大鳥居 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|本殿 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|いなり白狐社 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|社務所 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|宝物殿 ==文化財== === [[群馬県指定文化財一覧|群馬県指定文化財]] === 群馬県指定重要文化財 *最上流[[算額]]文化11年銘　附関流算額文化9年銘（昭和51年5月7日指定）{{Cite web |title=最上流算額文化11年銘　附関流算額文化9年銘 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4383.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 冠稲荷神社には3面の算額が奉納されており、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]9年（[[1812年]]）3月に関流の金井良之が奉納したものと、文化11年（[[1814年]]）に最上流の大川栄信門人の大川直信ら3名が奉納したものが文化財指定を受けている{{Cite book|和書 |title=太田市史 |date=1992-3-31 |year=1992 |publisher=太田市 |ref=harv |editor=太田市 |volume=通史編 近世 |doi=10.11501/9644639 |pages=607-612}}{{要登録}}。 群馬県指定天然記念物 * 冠稲荷のボケ（昭和30年1月14日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷のボケ - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 樹高約3.5ｍ、根元回り約3mの緋ボケで、約500本の樹が分岐して半円形状に叢生し株を形成している。3月中旬から4月上旬にかけて開花し秋に結実する。樹齢は300～400年と推定されている。昭和30年1月14日指定。群馬県文化財保護条例第8条の規定による。指定等の基準第6の3の（2）のアによる。園芸種の緋ボケでは県内でも古く株も大きい。 === 太田市指定文化財 === 太田市指定重要文化財 * 冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮（昭和47年9月26日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 本殿は三間社[[流造]]、千鳥破風[[向拝]]唐破風付、[[享保]]7年（[[1767年]]）の建築。聖天宮は四方[[入母屋造]]唐破風付の瓦葺で、[[安政]]4年（[[1857年]]）の建築{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。 * 冠稲荷神社の拝殿（平成2年3月26日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷神社の拝殿 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4401.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - [[寛政]]11年（[[1799年]]）の建築{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。 太田市指定重要無形民俗文化財 * 細谷冠稲荷の[[獅子舞]]（平成6年3月25日指定）{{Cite web |title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 新田義貞が重箱獅子を奉納したことに由来するとされ、[[初午]]大祭で奉納される。一時中断したが再興された。系統は箱田流とされ、一人立ち3頭獅子で雄獅子、雌獅子、法眼から成る{{Sfn|太田市|1985|pp=57-58,337-339}}。 ==アクセス== ===自動車=== *[[国道354号]][[東毛広域幹線道路|太田バイパス]]沿い *[[東北自動車道]][[館林インターチェンジ|館林IC]]より約40分 *[[関越自動車道]][[花園インターチェンジ|花園IC]]より約30分 *[[北関東自動車道]][[伊勢崎インターチェンジ|伊勢崎IC]]より約20分 ===電車=== *[[東武伊勢崎線]] **[[太田駅 (群馬県)|太田駅]]よりタクシーで15分 **[[細谷駅 (群馬県)|細谷駅]]より徒歩20分 == 周辺 == * [[太田市立沢野小学校]] * 国指定史跡「[[高山彦九郎]]宅跡附遺髪塚」 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=太田市史 |date=1985-3-30 |year=1985 |publisher=太田市 |ref=harv |editor=太田市 |volume=通史編 民俗（下巻） |doi=10.11501/9643541}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |ref=harv}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat}} * {{Official website}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんむりいなりしんしや}} [[category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]] [[Category:太田市の建築物]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:群馬県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:群馬県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:算額]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{神社 |名称 = 冠稲荷神社 |画像 = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[太田市]]細谷町1 |ISO = JP-13 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 16|緯度秒 = 7.69 |経度度 =139|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 5.49 |祭神 = {{要出典範囲|稲荷大神（[[宇迦之御魂大神]]ほか4柱の総称）|date=2024年8月}}など |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = [[1125年]]（[[天治]]2年） |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]正面千鳥破風向拝唐破風付 |例祭 = [[初午]]大祭（[[3月]]下旬の日曜日） |神事 = 初午大祭三大神事（稲荷神餅祭、義経公・義貞公厄除稚児行列、細谷冠稲荷獅子舞）、 [[新嘗祭]]（11月23日） |地図 = Japan Gunma |ラベル位置 = left }} '''冠稲荷神社'''（かんむりいなりじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]][[細谷町 (太田市)|細谷町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[稲荷神#日本七社|日本七社]]（日本七稲荷）の一つを称する{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/ |title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社 |publisher='''冠稲荷神社''' |accessdate=2018-11-26 }}。かつては[[養蚕業|養蚕]]の神として崇められ、旧[[社格]]は村社{{Sfn|太田市|1985|pp=42,57-58}}。 == 祭神 == *[[宇迦御魂神]]{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}} *[[大穴牟遅神]] *[[太田神]] *[[大宮能売神]] *[[保食命]] *[[少彦名神]] *[[応神天皇|品陀和気神]] *[[菊理姫|菊理日売神]] *[[市杵島姫|市杵島毘売神]] *[[大物主神]] *[[菅原道真|菅原道眞公]] *[[建御名方神|健御名方神]] *[[スサノオ|素盞鳴神]] *[[奇稲田姫|奇稲田比賣神]] *[[天照大御神]] *[[祓戸大神|祓戸四神]] *[[薬師如来|薬師菩薩明神]] == 概要 == [[1125年]]（[[天治]]2年）、[[源義国]]による創建と伝わる{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。 [[1174年]]（[[承安 (日本)|承安]]4年）、[[源義経]]が奥州に下向する際、源氏ゆかりの社であることを知り、冠の中に勧請してきた[[伏見稲荷大社]]の分霊を鎮祭したことにより冠稲荷と呼ばれるとされる{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|太田市|1985|pp=681-682}}。 [[1333年]]（[[元弘]]3年）、[[新田義貞]]が挙兵の際戦勝祈願を行い、勝利を収めたため重箱獅子を奉納、[[キンモクセイ]]を植え、[[神領]]を寄進したという{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。 境内に咲く県指定天然記念物の[[ボケ (植物)|木瓜]]の花は有名。 == 境内 == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|甲（きのえ）大鳥居 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|本殿 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|いなり白狐社 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|社務所 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|宝物殿 ==文化財== === [[群馬県指定文化財一覧|群馬県指定文化財]] === 群馬県指定重要文化財 *最上流[[算額]]文化11年銘　附関流算額文化9年銘（昭和51年5月7日指定）{{Cite web |title=最上流算額文化11年銘　附関流算額文化9年銘 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4383.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 冠稲荷神社には3面の算額が奉納されており、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]9年（[[1812年]]）3月に関流の金井良之が奉納したものと、文化11年（[[1814年]]）に最上流の大川栄信門人の大川直信ら3名が奉納したものが文化財指定を受けている{{Cite book|和書 |title=太田市史 |date=1992-3-31 |year=1992 |publisher=太田市 |ref=harv |editor=太田市 |volume=通史編 近世 |doi=10.11501/9644639 |pages=607-612}}{{要登録}}。 群馬県指定天然記念物 * 冠稲荷のボケ（昭和30年1月14日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷のボケ - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 樹高約3.5ｍ、根元回り約3mの緋ボケで、約500本の樹が分岐して半円形状に叢生し株を形成している。3月中旬から4月上旬にかけて開花し秋に結実する。樹齢は300～400年と推定されている。昭和30年1月14日指定。群馬県文化財保護条例第8条の規定による。指定等の基準第6の3の（2）のアによる。園芸種の緋ボケでは県内でも古く株も大きい。 === 太田市指定文化財 === 太田市指定重要文化財 * 冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮（昭和47年9月26日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 本殿は三間社[[流造]]、千鳥破風[[向拝]]唐破風付、[[享保]]7年（[[1767年]]）の建築。聖天宮は四方[[入母屋造]]唐破風付の瓦葺で、[[安政]]4年（[[1857年]]）の建築{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。 * 冠稲荷神社の拝殿（平成2年3月26日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷神社の拝殿 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4401.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - [[寛政]]11年（[[1799年]]）の建築{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。 太田市指定重要無形民俗文化財 * 細谷冠稲荷の[[獅子舞]]（平成6年3月25日指定）{{Cite web |title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 新田義貞が重箱獅子を奉納したことに由来するとされ、[[初午]]大祭で奉納される。一時中断したが再興された。系統は箱田流とされ、一人立ち3頭獅子で雄獅子、雌獅子、法眼から成る{{Sfn|太田市|1985|pp=57-58,337-339}}。 ==アクセス== ===自動車=== *[[国道354号]][[東毛広域幹線道路|太田バイパス]]沿い *[[東北自動車道]][[館林インターチェンジ|館林IC]]より約40分 *[[関越自動車道]][[花園インターチェンジ|花園IC]]より約30分 *[[北関東自動車道]][[伊勢崎インターチェンジ|伊勢崎IC]]より約20分 ===電車=== *[[東武伊勢崎線]] **[[太田駅 (群馬県)|太田駅]]よりタクシーで15分 **[[細谷駅 (群馬県)|細谷駅]]より徒歩20分 == 周辺 == * [[太田市立沢野小学校]] * 国指定史跡「[[高山彦九郎]]宅跡附遺髪塚」 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=太田市史 |date=1985-3-30 |year=1985 |publisher=太田市 |ref=harv |editor=太田市 |volume=通史編 民俗（下巻） |doi=10.11501/9643541}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |ref=harv}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat}} * {{Official website}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんむりいなりしんしや}} [[category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]] [[Category:太田市の建築物]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:群馬県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:群馬県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:算額]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

revision.comment: 群馬県指定重要文化財を追加

太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 本殿は三間社[[流造]]、千鳥破風[[向拝]]唐破風付、[[享保]]7年（[[1767年]]）の建築。聖天宮は四方[[入母屋造]]唐破風付の瓦葺で、[[安政]]4年（[[1857年]]）の建築{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。2024年に県指定重要文化財指定を県知事に答申。{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷神社（群馬・太田市）を県重文に 県文化財保護審が山本知事へ答申 牛田廃寺跡（藤岡）は県史跡に {{!}} 上毛新聞社のニュースサイト |url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830 |website=冠稲荷神社（群馬・太田市）を県重文に　県文化財保護審が山本知事へ答申　牛田廃寺跡（藤岡）は県史跡に {{!}} 上毛新聞社のニュースサイト |date=2024-08-02 |access-date=2024-10-31 |language=ja}} * 冠稲荷神社の拝殿（平成2年3月26日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷神社の拝殿 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4401.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - [[寛政]]11年（[[1799年]]）の建築{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。2024年に県指定重要文化財指定を県知事に答申。 *最上流[[算額]]文化11年銘　附関流算額文化9年銘（昭和51年5月7日指定）{{Cite web |title=最上流算額文化11年銘　附関流算額文化9年銘 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4383.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 冠稲荷神社には3面の算額が奉納されており、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]9年（[[1812年]]）3月に関流の金井良之が奉納したものと、文化11年（[[1814年]]）に最上流の大川栄信門人の大川直信ら3名が奉納したものが文化財指定を受けている{{Cite book|和書 |title=太田市史 |date=1992-3-31 |year=1992 |publisher=太田市 |ref=harv |editor=太田市 |volume=通史編 近世 |doi=10.11501/9644639 |pages=607-612}}{{要登録}}。 群馬県指定天然記念物 * 冠稲荷のボケ（昭和30年1月14日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷のボケ - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 樹高約3.5ｍ、根元回り約3mの緋ボケで、約500本の樹が分岐して半円形状に叢生し株を形成している。3月中旬から4月上旬にかけて開花し秋に結実する。樹齢は300～400年と推定されている。昭和30年1月14日指定。群馬県文化財保護条例第8条の規定による。指定等の基準第6の3の（2）のアによる。園芸種の緋ボケでは県内でも古く株も大きい。 === 太田市指定文化財 === 太田市指定重要無形民俗文化財 * 細谷冠稲荷の[[獅子舞]]（平成6年3月25日指定）{{Cite web |title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 新田義貞が重箱獅子を奉納したことに由来するとされ、[[初午]]大祭で奉納される。一時中断したが再興された。系統は箱田流とされ、一人立ち3頭獅子で雄獅子、雌獅子、法眼から成る{{Sfn|太田市|1985|pp=57-58,337-339}}。 ==アクセス== ===自動車=== *[[国道354号]][[東毛広域幹線道路|太田バイパス]]沿い *[[東北自動車道]][[館林インターチェンジ|館林IC]]より約40分 *[[関越自動車道]][[花園インターチェンジ|花園IC]]より約30分 *[[北関東自動車道]][[伊勢崎インターチェンジ|伊勢崎IC]]より約20分 ===電車=== *[[東武伊勢崎線]] **[[太田駅 (群馬県)|太田駅]]よりタクシーで15分 **[[細谷駅 (群馬県)|細谷駅]]より徒歩20分 == 周辺 == * [[太田市立沢野小学校]] * 国指定史跡「[[高山彦九郎]]宅跡附遺髪塚」 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=太田市史 |date=1985-3-30 |year=1985 |publisher=太田市 |ref=harv |editor=太田市 |volume=通史編 民俗（下巻） |doi=10.11501/9643541}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |ref=harv}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat}} * {{Official website}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんむりいなりしんしや}} [[category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]] [[Category:太田市の建築物]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:群馬県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:群馬県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:算額]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

revision.comment:
Created page with "{{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall

deciding to rename this category to a more descriptive one
some pages here lack the tag but to my knowledge everything here eitherr is an original page or at least worth preserving while I potentially trirm the wikipedia derived articles

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸四神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174年}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333年}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall

After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸四神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174年}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333年}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸四神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174年}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333年}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == revision.comment: 1 revision imported: inserting initial edit on these so the draft system sends me talk messages instead of dummy user
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸四神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174年}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333年}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸四神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174年}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333年}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸四神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174年}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333年}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸四神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174年}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333年}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸四神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174年}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333年}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸四神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174年}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333年}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸四神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174年}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333年}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering.
