{{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174年}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333年}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174年}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year }} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year }}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4年}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125年}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year }} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year }}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year }} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year }}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year }}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year }} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year }} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year }} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year }}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year }} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year }}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7年}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year }}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year }} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year }} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year }} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year }}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year }} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year }}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year }}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year }}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year }}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year }}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year }} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year }} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year }} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year }}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year }} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year }}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year }}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year }}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year }} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year }} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year }}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year }} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year }}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year }} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year }}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year }}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year }}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Sonsha]] [[Category:History of Ota City]] [[Category:Buildings in Ota City]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Gunma]] [[Category:Sangaku]] [[Category:Municipally designated tangible cultural properties in Gunma]] [[Category:祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[en:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanmuri Inari Shrine}}

It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=draft}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year|12=draft}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year|12=draft}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year|12=draft}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞|12=draft}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=draft}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year|12=draft}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=draft}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号|12=draft}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=draft}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=draft}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=draft}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線|12=draft}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅|12=draft}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅|12=draft}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=draft}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Sonsha]] [[Category:History of Ota City]] [[Category:Buildings in Ota City]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Gunma]] [[Category:Sangaku]] [[Category:Municipally designated tangible cultural properties in Gunma]] [[Category:祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[en:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanmuri Inari Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=draft}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=draft}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year|12=draft}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=draft}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=draft}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year|12=draft}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year|12=draft}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year|12=draft}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞|12=draft}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=draft}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year|12=draft}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=draft}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号|12=draft}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=draft}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=draft}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=draft}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線|12=draft}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅|12=draft}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅|12=draft}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=draft}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Sonsha]] [[Category:History of Ota City]] [[Category:Buildings in Ota City]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Gunma]] [[Category:Sangaku]] [[Category:Municipally designated tangible cultural properties in Gunma]] [[Category:祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[en:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanmuri Inari Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=draft|12=draft}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft|12=draft}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=draft|12=draft}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft|12=draft}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft|12=draft}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=draft|12=draft}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft|12=draft}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft|12=draft}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft|12=draft}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=draft|12=draft}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft|12=draft}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft|12=draft}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=draft|12=draft}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft|12=draft}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} under Japan's modern shrine ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=draft|12=draft}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft|12=draft}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft|12=draft}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=draft|12=draft}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft|12=draft}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft|12=draft}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=draft|12=draft}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft|12=draft}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft|12=draft}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=draft|12=draft}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft|12=draft}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft|12=draft}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=draft|12=draft}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号|12=draft|12=draft}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅|12=draft|12=draft}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=draft|12=draft}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Sonsha]] [[Category:History of Ota City]] [[Category:Buildings in Ota City]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Gunma]] [[Category:Sangaku]] [[Category:Municipally designated tangible cultural properties in Gunma]] [[Category:祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[en:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanmuri Inari Shrine}}
