Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=draft|12=draft}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号|12=draft|12=draft}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅|12=draft|12=draft}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=draft|12=draft}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Sonsha]] [[Category:History of Ota City]] [[Category:Buildings in Ota City]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Gunma]] [[Category:Sangaku]] [[Category:Municipally designated tangible cultural properties in Gunma]] [[Category:祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[en:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanmuri Inari Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=draft|12=draft}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft|12=draft}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=draft|12=draft}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft|12=draft}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft|12=draft}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=draft|12=draft}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft|12=draft}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft|12=draft}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=draft|12=draft}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号|12=draft|12=draft}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅|12=draft|12=draft}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=draft|12=draft}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Sonsha]] [[Category:History of Ota City]] [[Category:Buildings in Ota City]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Gunma]] [[Category:Sangaku]] [[Category:Municipally designated tangible cultural properties in Gunma]] [[Category:祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[en:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanmuri Inari Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=draft}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=draft}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year|12=draft}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=draft}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=draft}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year|12=draft}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year|12=draft}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year|12=draft}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞|12=draft}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=draft}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year|12=draft}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=draft}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=draft}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号|12=draft}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=draft}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=draft}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=draft}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線|12=draft}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅|12=draft}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅|12=draft}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=draft}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Sonsha]] [[Category:History of Ota City]] [[Category:Buildings in Ota City]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Gunma]] [[Category:Sangaku]] [[Category:Municipally designated tangible cultural properties in Gunma]] [[Category:祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[en:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanmuri Inari Shrine}}

''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Sonsha]] [[Category:History of Ota City]] [[Category:Buildings in Ota City]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Gunma]] [[Category:Sangaku]] [[Category:Municipally designated tangible cultural properties in Gunma]] [[Category:祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[en:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanmuri Inari Shrine}}

revision.comment: 32 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:冠稲荷神社 up to rev 102405581
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 冠稲荷神社 |画像 = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[太田市]]細谷町1 |ISO = JP-13 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 16|緯度秒 = 7.69 |経度度 =139|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 5.49 |祭神 = {{要出典範囲|稲荷大神（[[宇迦之御魂大神]]ほか4柱の総称）|date=2024年8月}}など |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = [[1125年]]（[[天治]]2年） |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]正面千鳥破風向拝唐破風付 |例祭 = [[初午]]大祭（[[3月]]下旬の日曜日） |神事 = 初午大祭三大神事（稲荷神餅祭、義経公・義貞公厄除稚児行列、細谷冠稲荷獅子舞）、 [[新嘗祭]]（11月23日） |地図 = Japan Gunma |ラベル位置 = left }} '''冠稲荷神社'''（かんむりいなりじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]][[細谷町 (太田市)|細谷町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[稲荷神#日本七社|日本七社]]（日本七稲荷）の一つを称する{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/ |title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社 |publisher='''冠稲荷神社''' |accessdate=2018-11-26 }}。かつては[[養蚕業|養蚕]]の神として崇められ、旧[[社格]]は村社{{Sfn|太田市|1985|pp=42,57-58}}。 == 祭神 == *[[宇迦御魂神]]{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}} *[[大穴牟遅神]] *[[太田神]] *[[大宮能売神]] *[[保食命]] *[[少彦名神]] *[[応神天皇|品陀和気神]] *[[菊理姫|菊理日売神]] *[[市杵島姫|市杵島毘売神]] *[[大物主神]] *[[菅原道真|菅原道眞公]] *[[建御名方神|健御名方神]] *[[スサノオ|素盞鳴神]] *[[奇稲田姫|奇稲田比賣神]] *[[天照大御神]] *[[祓戸大神|祓戸四神]] *[[薬師如来|薬師菩薩明神]] == 概要 == [[1125年]]（[[天治]]2年）、[[源義国]]による創建と伝わる{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。 [[1174年]]（[[承安 (日本)|承安]]4年）、[[源義経]]が奥州に下向する際、源氏ゆかりの社であることを知り、冠の中に勧請してきた[[伏見稲荷大社]]の分霊を鎮祭したことにより冠稲荷と呼ばれるとされる{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|太田市|1985|pp=681-682}}。 [[1333年]]（[[元弘]]3年）、[[新田義貞]]が挙兵の際戦勝祈願を行い、勝利を収めたため重箱獅子を奉納、[[キンモクセイ]]を植え、[[神領]]を寄進したという{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。 境内に咲く県指定天然記念物の[[ボケ (植物)|木瓜]]の花は有名。 == 境内 == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|甲（きのえ）大鳥居 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|本殿 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|いなり白狐社 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|社務所 File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|宝物殿 ==文化財== === [[群馬県指定文化財一覧|群馬県指定文化財]] === 群馬県指定重要文化財 *冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮（昭和47年9月26日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 本殿は三間社[[流造]]、千鳥破風[[向拝]]唐破風付、[[享保]]7年（[[1767年]]）の建築。聖天宮は四方[[入母屋造]]唐破風付の瓦葺で、[[安政]]4年（[[1857年]]）の建築{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。2024年に県指定重要文化財指定を県知事に答申。{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷神社（群馬・太田市）を県重文に 県文化財保護審が山本知事へ答申 牛田廃寺跡（藤岡）は県史跡に {{!}} 上毛新聞社のニュースサイト |url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830 |website=冠稲荷神社（群馬・太田市）を県重文に　県文化財保護審が山本知事へ答申　牛田廃寺跡（藤岡）は県史跡に {{!}} 上毛新聞社のニュースサイト |date=2024-08-02 |access-date=2024-10-31 |language=ja}} * 冠稲荷神社の拝殿（平成2年3月26日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷神社の拝殿 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4401.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - [[寛政]]11年（[[1799年]]）の建築{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。2024年に県指定重要文化財指定を県知事に答申。 *最上流[[算額]]文化11年銘　附関流算額文化9年銘（昭和51年5月7日指定）{{Cite web |title=最上流算額文化11年銘　附関流算額文化9年銘 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4383.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 冠稲荷神社には3面の算額が奉納されており、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]9年（[[1812年]]）3月に関流の金井良之が奉納したものと、文化11年（[[1814年]]）に最上流の大川栄信門人の大川直信ら3名が奉納したものが文化財指定を受けている{{Cite book|和書 |title=太田市史 |date=1992-3-31 |year=1992 |publisher=太田市 |ref=harv |editor=太田市 |volume=通史編 近世 |doi=10.11501/9644639 |pages=607-612}}{{要登録}}。 群馬県指定天然記念物 * 冠稲荷のボケ（昭和30年1月14日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷のボケ - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 樹高約3.5ｍ、根元回り約3mの緋ボケで、約500本の樹が分岐して半円形状に叢生し株を形成している。3月中旬から4月上旬にかけて開花し秋に結実する。樹齢は300～400年と推定されている。昭和30年1月14日指定。群馬県文化財保護条例第8条の規定による。指定等の基準第6の3の（2）のアによる。園芸種の緋ボケでは県内でも古く株も大きい。 === 太田市指定文化財 === 太田市指定重要無形民俗文化財 * 細谷冠稲荷の[[獅子舞]]（平成6年3月25日指定）{{Cite web |title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 新田義貞が重箱獅子を奉納したことに由来するとされ、[[初午]]大祭で奉納される。一時中断したが再興された。系統は箱田流とされ、一人立ち3頭獅子で雄獅子、雌獅子、法眼から成る{{Sfn|太田市|1985|pp=57-58,337-339}}。 ==アクセス== ===自動車=== *[[国道354号]][[東毛広域幹線道路|太田バイパス]]沿い *[[東北自動車道]][[館林インターチェンジ|館林IC]]より約40分 *[[関越自動車道]][[花園インターチェンジ|花園IC]]より約30分 *[[北関東自動車道]][[伊勢崎インターチェンジ|伊勢崎IC]]より約20分 ===電車=== *[[東武伊勢崎線]] **[[太田駅 (群馬県)|太田駅]]よりタクシーで15分 **[[細谷駅 (群馬県)|細谷駅]]より徒歩20分 == 周辺 == * [[太田市立沢野小学校]] * 国指定史跡「[[高山彦九郎]]宅跡附遺髪塚」 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=太田市史 |date=1985-3-30 |year=1985 |publisher=太田市 |ref=harv |editor=太田市 |volume=通史編 民俗（下巻） |doi=10.11501/9643541}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |ref=harv}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat}} * {{Official website}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんむりいなりしんしや}} [[category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]] [[Category:太田市の建築物]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:群馬県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:群馬県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:算額]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[冠稲荷神社]] to [[Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 本殿は三間社[[流造]]、千鳥破風[[向拝]]唐破風付、[[享保]]7年（[[1767年]]）の建築。聖天宮は四方[[入母屋造]]唐破風付の瓦葺で、[[安政]]4年（[[1857年]]）の建築{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。2024年に県指定重要文化財指定を県知事に答申。{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷神社（群馬・太田市）を県重文に 県文化財保護審が山本知事へ答申 牛田廃寺跡（藤岡）は県史跡に {{!}} 上毛新聞社のニュースサイト |url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830 |website=冠稲荷神社（群馬・太田市）を県重文に　県文化財保護審が山本知事へ答申　牛田廃寺跡（藤岡）は県史跡に {{!}} 上毛新聞社のニュースサイト |date=2024-08-02 |access-date=2024-10-31 |language=ja}} * 冠稲荷神社の拝殿（平成2年3月26日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷神社の拝殿 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4401.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - [[寛政]]11年（[[1799年]]）の建築{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=350-354}}。2024年に県指定重要文化財指定を県知事に答申。 *最上流[[算額]]文化11年銘　附関流算額文化9年銘（昭和51年5月7日指定）{{Cite web |title=最上流算額文化11年銘　附関流算額文化9年銘 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4383.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 冠稲荷神社には3面の算額が奉納されており、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]9年（[[1812年]]）3月に関流の金井良之が奉納したものと、文化11年（[[1814年]]）に最上流の大川栄信門人の大川直信ら3名が奉納したものが文化財指定を受けている{{Cite book|和書 |title=太田市史 |date=1992-3-31 |year=1992 |publisher=太田市 |ref=harv |editor=太田市 |volume=通史編 近世 |doi=10.11501/9644639 |pages=607-612}}{{要登録}}。 群馬県指定天然記念物 * 冠稲荷のボケ（昭和30年1月14日指定）{{Cite web |title=冠稲荷のボケ - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 樹高約3.5ｍ、根元回り約3mの緋ボケで、約500本の樹が分岐して半円形状に叢生し株を形成している。3月中旬から4月上旬にかけて開花し秋に結実する。樹齢は300～400年と推定されている。昭和30年1月14日指定。群馬県文化財保護条例第8条の規定による。指定等の基準第6の3の（2）のアによる。園芸種の緋ボケでは県内でも古く株も大きい。 === 太田市指定文化財 === 太田市指定重要無形民俗文化財 * 細谷冠稲荷の[[獅子舞]]（平成6年3月25日指定）{{Cite web |title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞 - 太田市ホームページ（文化財課） |url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html |website=www.city.ota.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}} - 新田義貞が重箱獅子を奉納したことに由来するとされ、[[初午]]大祭で奉納される。一時中断したが再興された。系統は箱田流とされ、一人立ち3頭獅子で雄獅子、雌獅子、法眼から成る{{Sfn|太田市|1985|pp=57-58,337-339}}。 ==アクセス== ===自動車=== *[[国道354号]][[東毛広域幹線道路|太田バイパス]]沿い *[[東北自動車道]][[館林インターチェンジ|館林IC]]より約40分 *[[関越自動車道]][[花園インターチェンジ|花園IC]]より約30分 *[[北関東自動車道]][[伊勢崎インターチェンジ|伊勢崎IC]]より約20分 ===電車=== *[[東武伊勢崎線]] **[[太田駅 (群馬県)|太田駅]]よりタクシーで15分 **[[細谷駅 (群馬県)|細谷駅]]より徒歩20分 == 周辺 == * [[太田市立沢野小学校]] * 国指定史跡「[[高山彦九郎]]宅跡附遺髪塚」 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=太田市史 |date=1985-3-30 |year=1985 |publisher=太田市 |ref=harv |editor=太田市 |volume=通史編 民俗（下巻） |doi=10.11501/9643541}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |ref=harv}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat}} * {{Official website}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんむりいなりしんしや}} [[category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]] [[Category:太田市の建築物]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:群馬県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:群馬県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:算額]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | established = {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|ja|3月}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道眞公}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|ja|1125th year}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2nd year}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|ja|源義国}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|ja|1174th year}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4th year}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari Taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|ja|1333rd year}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3rd year}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|ja|享保7th year}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|ja|安政4th year}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|National Route 354|ja|国道354号}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ota Station (Gunma)|ja|太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|ja|細谷駅}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Sonsha]] [[Category:History of Ota City]] [[Category:Buildings in Ota City]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Gunma]] [[Category:Sangaku]] [[Category:Municipally designated tangible cultural properties in Gunma]] [[Category:祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] [[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[en:Kanmuri Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|冠稲荷神社|version=102405581|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in [[:en:1125|1125]] ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579683}}) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshikuni|Minamoto no Yoshikuni]].{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In [[:en:1174|1174]] ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579684}}), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]] in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In [[:en:1333|1333]] ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579685}}), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|qq=|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|qq=|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in [[:en:1767|1767]], featuring three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] with Karahafu roof, built in [[:en:1857|1857]].{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|qq=|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|qq=|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = [[:en:1125|1125]] ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579683}}) | architecture = Three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late [[:en:March|March]]), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya|qq=|1=Hosoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501215}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in [[:en:1125|1125]] ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshikuni|Minamoto no Yoshikuni]].{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In [[:en:1174|1174]] ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684}}), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]] in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In [[:en:1333|1333]] ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685}}), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|qq=|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|qq=|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in [[:en:1767|1767]], featuring three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] with Karahafu roof, built in [[:en:1857|1857]].{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|qq=|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|qq=|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 354|National Route 354]] Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebayashi IC|qq=|1=Tatebayashi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666955}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanazono IC|qq=|1=Hanazono interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615194}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki IC|qq=|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tobu Isesaki Line]]: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from [[:en:太田駅|Ota Station (Gunma)]] ''' 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hosoya Station|Hosoya Station]] == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawano Elementary School|qq=|1=Sawano Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86176292}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and founded in [[:en:1125|1125]] ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) by 4|ja|承安4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684}}), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]] in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In [[:en:1333|1333]] ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685}}), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|qq=|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|qq=|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in [[:en:1767|1767]], featuring three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] with Karahafu roof, built in [[:en:1857|1857]].{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|qq=|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = [[:en:1125|1125]] ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) | architecture = Three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late [[:en:March|March]]), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya|1=Hosoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501215}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = [[:en:1125|1125]] ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) | architecture = Three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late [[:en:March|March]]), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya|1=Hosoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501215}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in [[:en:1125|1125]] ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshikuni|Minamoto no Yoshikuni]].{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In [[:en:1174|1174]] ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684}}), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]] in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In [[:en:1333|1333]] ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685}}), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in [[:en:1767|1767]], featuring three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] with Karahafu roof, built in [[:en:1857|1857]].{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 354|National Route 354]] Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebayashi IC|1=Tatebayashi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666955}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanazono IC|1=Hanazono interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615194}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki IC|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tobu Isesaki Line]]: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from [[:en:太田駅|Ota Station (Gunma)]] ''' 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hosoya Station|Hosoya Station]] == Nearby Attractions == * link invalid|WD=Q135579684}}), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]] in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In [[:en:1333|1333]] ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685}}), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in [[:en:1767|1767]], featuring three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] with Karahafu roof, built in [[:en:1857|1857]].{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 354|National Route 354]] Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebayashi IC|1=Tatebayashi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666955}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanazono IC|1=Hanazono interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615194}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki IC|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tobu Isesaki Line]]: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from [[:en:太田駅|Ota Station (Gunma)]] ''' 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hosoya Station|Hosoya Station]] == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawano Elementary School|1=Sawano Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86176292}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links [[sn:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11395055]] Wikidata name: Kanmuri Inari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11395055]] Wikidata name: Kanmuri Inari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = [[:en:1125|1125]] ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) | architecture = Three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late [[:en:March|March]]), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya|1=Hosoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501215}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太田命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太田命}} * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukemochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|1=Ukemochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2280323}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道眞公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in [[:en:1125|1125]] ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshikuni|Minamoto no Yoshikuni]].{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In [[:en:1174|1174]] ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684}}), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]] in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In [[:en:1333|1333]] ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685}}), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in [[:en:1767|1767]], featuring three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] with Karahafu roof, built in [[:en:1857|1857]].{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 354|National Route 354]] Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebayashi IC|1=Tatebayashi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666955}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanazono IC|1=Hanazono interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615194}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki IC|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tobu Isesaki Line]]: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from [[:en:太田駅|Ota Station (Gunma)]] ''' 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hosoya Station|Hosoya Station]] == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawano Elementary School|1=Sawano Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86176292}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine
