[[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11395055]] Wikidata name: Kanmuri Inari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11395055]] Wikidata name: Kanmuri Inari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = [[:en:1125|1125]] ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) | architecture = Three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late [[:en:March|March]]), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya|1=Hosoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501215}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太田命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太田命}} * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukemochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|1=Ukemochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2280323}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道眞公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in [[:en:1125|1125]] ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshikuni|Minamoto no Yoshikuni]].{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In [[:en:1174|1174]] ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684}}), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]] in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In [[:en:1333|1333]] ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685}}), [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in [[:en:1767|1767]], featuring three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] with Karahafu roof, built in [[:en:1857|1857]].{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along [[:en:Japan National Route 354|National Route 354]] Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebayashi IC|1=Tatebayashi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666955}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanazono IC|1=Hanazono interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615194}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki IC|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tobu Isesaki Line]]: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from [[:en:太田駅|Ota Station (Gunma)]] ''' 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Hosoya Station|Hosoya Station]] == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawano Elementary School|1=Sawano Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86176292}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya|1=Hosoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501215}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道眞公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|en|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|WD=Q3314719|lt=Minamoto no Yoshikuni}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|en|1333|WD=Q6004|lt=1333}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|en|1767|WD=Q7672|lt=1767}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|en|1857|WD=Q7680|lt=1857}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|Japan National Route 354|en|Japan National Route 354|WD=Q6157321|lt=National Route 354}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebayashi IC|1=Tatebayashi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666955}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanazono IC|1=Hanazono interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615194}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki IC|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|en|Tobu Isesaki Line|WD=Q906598|lt=Tobu Isesaki Line}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|太田駅|en|太田駅|WD=Q1206882|lt=Ota Station (Gunma)}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|en|Hosoya Station|WD=Q857578|lt=Hosoya Station}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawano Elementary School|1=Sawano Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86176292}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|冠稲荷神社|version=102405581|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:冠稲荷神社]] {{Commons category|Kanmuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11395055}} [[Category:Buildings in Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Sangaku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11395055]] Wikidata name: Kanmuri Inari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11395055]] Wikidata name: Kanmuri Inari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya|1=Hosoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501215}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太田命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太田命}} * [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya|1=Hosoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501215}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太田命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太田命}} * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukemochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|1=Ukemochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2280323}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀和気神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道眞公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|en|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|WD=Q3314719|lt=Minamoto no Yoshikuni}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|en|1333|WD=Q6004|lt=1333}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|en|1767|WD=Q7672|lt=1767}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|en|1857|WD=Q7680|lt=1857}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|Japan National Route 354|en|Japan National Route 354|WD=Q6157321|lt=National Route 354}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebayashi IC|1=Tatebayashi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666955}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanazono IC|1=Hanazono interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615194}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki IC|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|en|Tobu Isesaki Line|WD=Q906598|lt=Tobu Isesaki Line}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|太田駅|en|太田駅|WD=Q1206882|lt=Ota Station (Gunma)}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|en|Hosoya Station|WD=Q857578|lt=Hosoya Station}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawano Elementary School|1=Sawano Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86176292}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|冠稲荷神社|version=102405581|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:冠稲荷神社]] {{Commons * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|en|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|WD=Q3314719|lt=Minamoto no Yoshikuni}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|en|1333|WD=Q6004|lt=1333}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|en|1767|WD=Q7672|lt=1767}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|en|1857|WD=Q7680|lt=1857}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|Japan National Route 354|en|Japan National Route 354|WD=Q6157321|lt=National Route 354}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebayashi IC|1=Tatebayashi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666955}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanazono IC|1=Hanazono interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615194}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki IC|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|en|Tobu Isesaki Line|WD=Q906598|lt=Tobu Isesaki Line}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|太田駅|en|太田駅|WD=Q1206882|lt=Ota Station (Gunma)}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|en|Hosoya Station|WD=Q857578|lt=Hosoya Station}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawano Elementary School|1=Sawano Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86176292}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya|1=Hosoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501215}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太田命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太田命}} * It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|en|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|WD=Q3314719|lt=Minamoto no Yoshikuni}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|en|1333|WD=Q6004|lt=1333}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|en|1767|WD=Q7672|lt=1767}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|en|1857|WD=Q7680|lt=1857}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|Japan National Route 354|en|Japan National Route 354|WD=Q6157321|lt=National Route 354}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebayashi IC|1=Tatebayashi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666955}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanazono IC|1=Hanazono interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615194}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki IC|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|en|Tobu Isesaki Line|WD=Q906598|lt=Tobu Isesaki Line}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|太田駅|en|太田駅|WD=Q1206882|lt=Ota Station (Gunma)}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|en|Hosoya Station|WD=Q857578|lt=Hosoya Station}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawano Elementary School|1=Sawano Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86176292}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links Yoshikuni|WD=Q3314719|lt=Minamoto no Yoshikuni}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|en|1333|WD=Q6004|lt=1333}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|en|1767|WD=Q7672|lt=1767}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|en|1857|WD=Q7680|lt=1857}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|Japan National Route 354|en|Japan National Route 354|WD=Q6157321|lt=National Route 354}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebayashi IC|1=Tatebayashi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666955}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanazono IC|1=Hanazono interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615194}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki IC|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|en|Tobu Isesaki Line|WD=Q906598|lt=Tobu Isesaki Line}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|太田駅|en|太田駅|WD=Q1206882|lt=Ota Station (Gunma)}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|en|Hosoya Station|WD=Q857578|lt=Hosoya Station}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawano Elementary School|1=Sawano Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86176292}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja|高山彦九郎宅跡附遺髪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Hikokurō Residence and Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.kanmuri.com|Kanmuri Inari Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|冠稲荷神社|version=102405581|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:冠稲荷神社]] {{Commons [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kanmuri-inari-jinja]][[ja:冠稲荷神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanmuri Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125|1=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683|1=1125}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya|1=Q27501215|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501215}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine Ranking system.{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=42,57-58}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太田命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太田命}} * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukemochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|1=Ukemochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2280323}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|ja|少彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Emperor {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|菊理日売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島毘売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|菅原道眞公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田比賣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師菩薩明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125|1=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683|1=1125}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|en|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|WD=Q3314719|lt=Minamoto no Yoshikuni|1=Minamoto no Yoshikuni}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174|1=1174}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684|1=1174}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|en|1333|WD=Q6004|lt=1333|1=1333}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685|1=1333}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|en|1767|WD=Q7672|lt=1767|1=1767}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|16|7.6|N|139|21|5.4|E}}}} | location = Hosoya-cho 1, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.2688 | longitude = 139.3515 | deity = {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}} (collective name for {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} and four other deities) | classification = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125|1=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683|1=1125}}) | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with decorative Chidori gable and Karahafu porch | festivals = Hatsuo Grand Festival (late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}}), Shinjo-sai (November 23) }} '''Kanmuri Inari Shrine''' (冠稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hosoya|ja|細谷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya|1=Q27501215|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501215}}, Ota City, Gunma Prefecture. It is regarded as one of the {{ill|Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja|日本七社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Great Inari Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q719665}}.{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社と日本七社|url=https://kanmuri.com/ka/jinjanituite/kannmuri_nanansya/|publisher=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|accessdate=2024-11-26}} Historically, the shrine was revered as a deity of sericulture. It is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under Japan's modern shrine Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125|1=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683|1=1125}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|en|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|WD=Q3314719|lt=Minamoto no Yoshikuni|1=Minamoto no Yoshikuni}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174|1=1174}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684|1=1174}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|en|1333|WD=Q6004|lt=1333|1=1333}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685|1=1333}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|en|1767|WD=Q7672|lt=1767|1=1767}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|en|1857|WD=Q7680|lt=1857|1=1857}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along {{ill|Japan National Route 354|en|Japan National Route 354|WD=Q6157321|lt=National Route 354|1=Japan National Route 354}} Ota Bypass * Approx. 40 minutes from {{ill|Tatebayashi IC|ja|館林インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebayashi IC|1=Tatebayashi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666955}} (Tohoku Expressway) * Approx. 30 minutes from {{ill|Hanazono IC|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanazono IC|1=Hanazono interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615194}} (Kan-Etsu Expressway) * Approx. 20 minutes from {{ill|Isesaki IC|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki IC|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} (Kita-Kanto Expressway) === By Train === * {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|en|Tobu Isesaki Line|WD=Q906598|lt=Tobu Isesaki Line|1=Tobu Isesaki Line}}: ''' 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|太田駅|en|太田駅|WD=Q1206882|lt=Ota Station (Gunma)|1=太田駅}} ''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hosoya Station|en|Hosoya Station|WD=Q857578|lt=Hosoya Station|1=Hosoya Station}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Sawano Elementary School|ja|太田市立沢野小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawano Elementary School|1=Q86176292|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86176292}} * National Historic Site: {{ill|Takayama Hikokurō Residence Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Overview == The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|1125|en|1125|WD=Q23799|lt=1125|1=1125}} ({{ill|Tenji 2|ja|天治2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579683|1=1125}}) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|en|Minamoto no Yoshikuni|WD=Q3314719|lt=Minamoto no Yoshikuni|1=Minamoto no Yoshikuni}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} In {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174|1=1174}} ({{ill|Jōan 4|ja|承安4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579684|1=1174}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} is believed to have enshrined a divided spirit from {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha}} in his crown upon learning of the shrine's Minamoto lineage, thus bestowing its name.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Sfn|Ota City|1985|pp=681-682}} In {{ill|1333|en|1333|WD=Q6004|lt=1333|1=1333}} ({{ill|Genkō 3|ja|元弘3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579685|1=1333}}), {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory at the shrine before launching his campaign. After his victory, he dedicated a ceremonial lion offering and planted fragrant {{ill|Kinmokusei|ja|キンモクセイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmokusei|1=Osmanthus fragrans var. aurantiacus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297955}} trees. He also donated land to the shrine.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}} The shrine is famous for its {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} flowers, designated as a natural monument by Gunma Prefecture. == Grounds == File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Kinoe-o-torii.jpg|Kinoe Grand Torii File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja Byakko-sha.jpg|Inari White Fox Shrine File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja shamusho.jpg|Shrine Office File:Kanmuri-inari-jinja homotsuden.jpg|Treasure Hall == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties:''' * Main Hall and Seitengū Shrine (Designated September 26, 1972){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社の本殿並びに聖天宮|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4399.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Main Hall: Built in {{ill|1767|en|1767|WD=Q7672|lt=1767|1=1767}}, featuring three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with Chidori gable and Karahafu porch. ''' Seitengū Shrine: Four-sided {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with Karahafu roof, built in {{ill|1857|en|1857|WD=Q7680|lt=1857|1=1857}}.{{Sfn|Gunma Cultural Affairs Department|2022|pp=350-354}}{{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷神社を県重文に答申|url=https://www.jomo-news.co.jp/articles/-/507830|publisher=Jomo Shimbun|accessdate=2024-08-02}} '''Natural Monuments:''' * {{ill|Boke (Chaenomeles)|ja|ボケ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boke (Chaenomeles)|1=Boke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q891202}} Trees (Designated January 14, 1955){{Cite web|ja|title=冠稲荷のボケ|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4397.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Estimated to be 300–400 years old, forming dense clusters over a semicircular area. Flowers bloom in mid-March to early April. === Ota City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Intangible Cultural Properties:''' * Hosoya Kanmuri Inari Lion Dance (Designated March 25, 1994){{Cite web|ja|title=細谷冠稲荷の獅子舞|url=https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/page/4496.html|publisher=Ota City|accessdate=2024-08-23}} ''' Associated with Nitta Yoshisada's ceremonial lion offering. Performed during the {{ill|Hatsuo|ja|初午|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuo|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q510847}} Grand Festival. == Access == === By Car === * Located along
